title: Mammillaria peninsularis
intro: Mammillaria peninsularis is a species of cactus native to Mexico, belonging to the family Cactaceae and characterized by its globular stem and fine spines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. Bioactive constituents found in Mammillaria peninsularis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and anxiety.
taxonomy: Mammillaria peninsularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family (Cactaceae) and belongs to the genus Mammillaria. It is a part of the subgenus Mammillaria, which is a group of cacti characterized by their small, globular bodies and short, fine spines. Within the subgenus, Mammillaria peninsularis is assigned to the Mammillaria section known as 'Pseudopachycereeae'. This section includes species that are typically found in the Americas and are known for their small, globular, or cylindrical stems. As part of the Cactaceae family, Mammillaria peninsularis shares characteristics with other cacti species, including the ability to store water in their stems.
